changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_enabled to k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_logged for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST 800-53 CA-7-c control for continuous monitoring. Audit logging and configuration change logging are critical components of this control. The naming was mostly consistent, but a minor change was made to better reflect the purpose of logging network policy changes.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the logging aspect, as it aligns with continuous monitoring.
title: Continuous Monitoring - Ongoing control assessments in accordance...
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 CA-7-c control focuses on continuous monitoring and ongoing control assessments. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled and properly configured to capture changes, such as network policies and admission controller configurations. Monitoring changes to network configurations can be achieved through audit logs and ensuring that NetworkPolicies are actively monitored. Admission controllers can be used to enforce policies and log changes to configurations.
